source.title: Executive Order N-7-26
source.notes: Scanned, untagged signed PDF. Short excerpts were independently transcribed and manually reviewed; the source image controls.

administrativeDirectives.excerpt: I FURTHER DIRECT that as soon as hereafter possible, this Order be filed in the Office of the Secretary of State and that widespread publicity and notice be given of this Order.

sourceContexts.excerpt: Caltrans is directed to implement recommendations documented in the 2025 Transit Transformation Task Force Report to formalize the California Integrated Mobility Program through the following actions:

sourceNotices.excerpt: This Order is not intended to, and does not, create any rights or benefits, substantive or procedural, enforceable at law or in equity, against the State of California, its agencies, departments, entities, officers, employees, or any other person.

organizations.name: California Department of Housing and Community Development

directives.label: 1(a)
directives.title: Statewide transit priority project list
directives.excerpt: develop a list of transit priority projects statewide that can be updated regularly

qualifiers.appliesTo: consistency with relevant local or regional transportation plans

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

analysis.summary: Create a regularly maintainable statewide list that connects transit-priority projects to location needs, applicable plans, and eligible funding.

expectedOutputs.text: A versioned statewide priority-project list with funding eligibility and plan crosswalks

dependencies.text: Stable project and location identifiers plus an update and approval process

openQuestions: What determines inclusion and priority?
What is the refresh and conflict-resolution process?

directives.label: 1(b)
directives.title: State mobility-management role
directives.excerpt: Establish Caltrans as a Mobility Manager, as defined by the Federal Transit Administration

qualifiers.text: as defined by the Federal Transit Administration
qualifiers.appliesTo: Caltrans Mobility Manager role

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

analysis.summary: Establish a statewide mobility-management role that coordinates trips and shared planning, scheduling, payment, and asset technologies across grantees.

expectedOutputs.text: A mobility-management operating model and portfolio of shared coordination tools

dependencies.text: Governance, data stewardship, procurement, cost allocation, and participant onboarding

openQuestions: Which formal action establishes the role?
Which services are shared, optional, or required?

directives.label: 1(c)
directives.title: Section 5307 direct-recipient option
directives.excerpt: Notify jurisdictions receiving FTA Section 5307 Urbanized Area Formula Program funding that Caltrans is available to serve as the direct recipient

qualifiers.text: if they elect to do so
qualifiers.appliesTo: jurisdictions choosing Caltrans as direct recipient

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

analysis.summary: Offer eligible Section 5307 jurisdictions the option to use Caltrans as direct recipient and coordinate resulting federal changes.

expectedOutputs.text: Jurisdiction notice, election workflow, and federal recipient-change process

dependencies.text: Eligible-jurisdiction register, recipient-readiness controls, and federal compliance procedures

openQuestions: How is “more rapid” disbursement measured?
How do compliance duties and reversibility work after an election?

directives.label: 1(d)
directives.title: Service-led State Transit Plan
directives.excerpt: future iterations of the State Transit Plan shall adopt a service-led planning approach that aligns with the California State Rail Plan

qualifiers.appliesTo: State Transit Plan adoption of a service-led planning approach

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

analysis.summary: Prepare future State Transit Plan iterations to use service-led planning aligned with the State Rail Plan.

expectedOutputs.text: A documented service-led planning method and rail-plan alignment crosswalk

dependencies.text: A shared definition of service-led planning and measurable alignment

openQuestions: What steps satisfy the directive before the next plan is published?
How will alignment be measured and approved?

directives.label: 1(e)
directives.title: Transit infrastructure design and permitting materials
directives.excerpt: Begin the process of developing design guidance for transit infrastructure on the state highway system

qualifiers.appliesTo: development of design guidance, standard plans and specifications, policy, and permitting procedures

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date
one calendar-year anniversary of the effective date

analysis.summary: Start and complete a coordinated set of design, specification, policy, and permitting materials for transit infrastructure on the State Highway System.

expectedOutputs.text: Published design guidance, standard plans and specifications, policies, procedures, and updated manuals

dependencies.text: Named document owners, adoption authorities, accessibility requirements, and alignment with traffic-control standards

openQuestions: Which exact documents are in scope?
What review and adoption process applies to each artifact?

directives.label: 1(f)
directives.title: CEQA transit-delivery report
directives.excerpt: Report to CalSTA how recent changes to the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) ... will be used to expedite transit projects

qualifiers.text: including but not limited to, Senate Bill 71 (Chapter 742, Statutes of 2025)
qualifiers.appliesTo: recent changes to CEQA covered by the report
use of recent CEQA changes to expedite transit projects

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

analysis.summary: Explain to the transportation agency how recent CEQA changes can be applied to expedite transit projects after consultation with local partners.

expectedOutputs.text: A report connecting recent CEQA changes to eligible transit-project pathways

dependencies.text: Legal and project-category crosswalk, local consultation record, and a baseline for expedited delivery

openQuestions: Will the report be public?
Which project categories and measures define expedited delivery?

directives.label: 1(g)
directives.title: Active transportation delivery support team
directives.excerpt: Establish a Statewide Active Transportation Delivery Support Team

qualifiers.appliesTo: Active Transportation Program projects supported by the team
partnering with local and regional agencies to provide additional project delivery capacity

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

analysis.summary: Create a statewide team that can add technical and delivery capacity for state-funded active-transportation projects.

expectedOutputs.text: A staffed support team with an intake, triage, assistance, and delivery-capacity model

dependencies.text: Charter, staffing and budget, project inventory, service levels, and outcome measures

openQuestions: Where will the team sit organizationally?
How are projects prioritized and how long does support last?

directives.title: Transit and rail funding dashboards
directives.excerpt: create real time dashboards that track the status of all available funding programs

qualifiers.appliesTo: the listed dashboard measures

qualifiers.text: as grant funding is consolidated into a system of record
qualifiers.appliesTo: addition of relevant performance measures

analysis.summary: Create funding-program dashboards covering applications, awards, contracting, reimbursement, and other performance measures to support transparency and equitable access.

expectedOutputs.text: Public dashboards and a documented metric/data model spanning available transit and rail funding programs

dependencies.text: Canonical program, cycle, application, award, contract, and invoice timestamps with public-data rules

openQuestions: Which programs and records are in scope?
What update interval and system of record support the term “real time”?

directives.label: 3(a)
directives.title: Federal formula-fund flexibility
directives.excerpt: Explore opportunities to support regions that reprogram Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) formula funds for transit uses

qualifiers.appliesTo: reprogramming FHWA formula funds for transit uses
matching state share dollars

analysis.summary: Explore lawful ways to help regions shift federal highway formula funds to transit, including possible state-share matching.

expectedOutputs.text: An opportunity and eligibility analysis for reprogramming and state-match support

dependencies.text: Program-by-program legal eligibility, state-match availability, and a regional project pipeline

openQuestions: What artifact completes the instruction to explore?
Which programs, match sources, and decision authorities are included?

directives.label: 3(b)
directives.title: Small-agency TDA and NTD reporting automation
directives.excerpt: Take steps to automate Transportation Development Act and National Transit Database reporting for rural and small agencies

qualifiers.appliesTo: Transportation Development Act and National Transit Database reporting
agencies covered by the reporting-automation action

analysis.summary: Take steps to automate Transportation Development Act and National Transit Database reporting for rural and small agencies.

expectedOutputs.text: A field-level reporting crosswalk, automation boundary, provenance model, and implementation roadmap

dependencies.text: Authoritative reporting definitions, source-system evidence, validation, audit trail, and submitter certification

openQuestions: How are rural, small, and automate defined?
Which fields can be derived, which can only be assisted, and who remains the legal submitter?

directives.label: 3(c)
directives.title: Faster and more flexible fund distribution
directives.excerpt: Identify and implement measures to reduce the timelines for distribution of funds wherever feasible

qualifiers.appliesTo: reducing timelines for distribution of funds
lifting limits imposed on use of funds
providing pre-award authority for federal funds

analysis.summary: Reduce fund-distribution timelines where feasible and examine additional flexibility for uses and federal pre-award authority.

expectedOutputs.text: Program-specific timeline-reduction and fund-flexibility measures

dependencies.text: Baseline and target timelines plus legal authority for each administrative or policy change

openQuestions: Which programs and process stages are covered?
Which changes are administrative, statutory, or federal?

directives.label: 3(d)
directives.title: Federal environmental-review assignment
directives.excerpt: Begin the process of obtaining a National Environmental Protection Act (NEPA) assignment from the U.S. Department of Transportation and Federal Transit Administration

qualifiers.appliesTo: obtaining the NEPA assignment

sourceNotes.text: The excerpt preserves the signed order's use of “National Environmental Protection Act (NEPA)” rather than silently changing the source wording.

analysis.summary: Begin pursuing federal environmental-review assignment for transit and commuter-rail projects, subject to federal law.

expectedOutputs.text: An assignment pathway with legal authority, agreements, staffing, and quality controls

dependencies.text: Federal eligibility, state authority, liability terms, and an assignment agreement

openQuestions: Which assignment mechanism and project classes are contemplated?
What state legislation or agreement would be required?

directives.label: 3(e)
directives.title: BRT and bus-lane technical assistance
directives.excerpt: Provide technical assistance to local agencies, where feasible, with the implementation of Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) / Bus Only lanes

qualifiers.appliesTo: technical assistance to local agencies for BRT, bus-only lanes, and associated infrastructure

qualifiers.text: where possible via the cooperative agreement process
qualifiers.appliesTo: assistance with planning, engineering, and implementation

analysis.summary: Offer feasible planning, engineering, implementation, and cooperative-agreement assistance for BRT and bus-only infrastructure on state highways.

expectedOutputs.text: A technical-assistance offer, intake route, and cooperative-agreement pathway

dependencies.text: Standard assistance scope, project intake, agreements, and design/permitting guidance

openQuestions: Is assistance request-driven or tied to the priority-project list?
What phases, service levels, and cost-sharing rules apply?

directives.label: 3(f)
directives.title: Digital real-property system, holdings digitization, and condition inventory
directives.excerpt: In coordination with the Department of General Services, create an implementation plan for the creation of a centralized, digital real property asset management system and fully digitize its real estate holdings, inventory buildings and their state of repair.

qualifiers.text: In coordination with the Department of General Services
qualifiers.appliesTo: the implementation plan, holdings digitization, and building repair-state inventory

analysis.summary: Create an implementation plan for a centralized digital real-property asset management system, fully digitize Caltrans real-estate holdings, and inventory buildings and their state of repair in coordination with DGS.

expectedOutputs.text: A delivery model linking the implementation plan, completed holdings digitization, and building repair-state inventory

dependencies.text: Canonical property identifiers, geospatial and condition standards, system ownership, and digitization rules

openQuestions: What completion criteria and quality controls define “fully digitize”?
Which holdings, buildings, and repair-state categories are in scope?

directives.label: 3(g)
directives.title: Airspace policy for zero-emission fleets
directives.excerpt: Identify updates to its existing exception policy around the use of Caltrans airspace

qualifiers.text: where vehicles are already being stored
qualifiers.appliesTo: operator-controlled land beneath or adjacent to Caltrans facilities

qualifiers.text: where consistent with state policy and safety and operational requirements
qualifiers.appliesTo: airspace exception-policy updates

analysis.summary: Identify airspace-policy changes that could allow existing operator-controlled storage sites to support zero-emission vehicle storage and energy infrastructure.

expectedOutputs.text: A documented set of exception-policy updates and eligible-site conditions

dependencies.text: Current policy, parcel and airspace inventory, safety and structural review, utilities, and operator agreements

openQuestions: Does identify include adoption and implementation?
How are operator control, existing storage, and eligible sites defined?

directives.label: 3(h)
directives.title: Excess toll-revenue guidance
directives.excerpt: provide guidance to toll operators on how to prioritize excess toll revenues for unmet transit operational and capital needs

qualifiers.text: in accordance with state and federal law
qualifiers.appliesTo: guidance on prioritizing excess toll revenues
Caltrans consideration of excess revenue for transit within planned managed-lane corridors

analysis.summary: Develop lawful guidance for directing excess toll revenue toward unmet transit operating and capital needs, including managed-lane corridors.

expectedOutputs.text: Guidance defining eligible needs, excess revenue, corridor relationship, and prioritization

dependencies.text: Toll-facility and revenue inventory plus state and federal legal restrictions

openQuestions: Is the guidance binding or advisory?
How are excess revenue, unmet need, and corridor benefit calculated?

directives.label: 3(i)
directives.title: Transit updates to the California MUTCD
directives.excerpt: Initiate an update to the state Manual of Uniform Traffic Control Devices (MUTCD) to address transit infrastructure

qualifiers.appliesTo: transit infrastructure addressed by the MUTCD update, including transit signal priority and bus lanes
federal approvals for the MUTCD updates

analysis.summary: Start a California traffic-control-manual update for transit infrastructure and seek any required federal approvals.

expectedOutputs.text: A scoped MUTCD update, stakeholder process, draft, approvals, and adoption path

dependencies.text: Manual change control, federal conformity, stakeholder review, and alignment with design standards

openQuestions: Which chapters and devices are in scope?
What draft, approval, and adoption milestones apply?

directives.label: 3(j)
directives.title: Prohousing transit-funding priority
directives.excerpt: develop funding criteria that prioritize funding to, and award processing for, transit projects located in a jurisdiction with a Pro-Housing Designation

qualifiers.text: In consultation with the Department of Housing and Community Development and CTC
qualifiers.appliesTo: development of Pro-Housing funding and award-processing criteria

collaboratorOrganizations: California Department of Housing and Community Development

analysis.summary: Develop criteria that prioritize both funding and award processing for transit projects in Prohousing-designated jurisdictions.

expectedOutputs.text: Program criteria and processing rules tied to an authoritative Prohousing designation record

dependencies.text: Designation data, effective dates, cross-jurisdiction rules, and program scoring authority

openQuestions: Which programs and weighting or queue mechanics are covered?
How are projects spanning jurisdictions and designation changes handled?

directives.title: CHSRA NEPA delegation and programmatic review
directives.excerpt: identify and implement opportunities to leverage CHSRA's Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) NEPA Delegation

qualifiers.appliesTo: undertaking programmatic environmental review

analysis.summary: Identify and implement opportunities to use CHSRA’s FRA NEPA Delegation for State Rail Plan projects, working with the Caltrans Division of Rail and, where possible, undertaking programmatic environmental review.

expectedOutputs.text: An eligibility and delivery pathway for candidate State Rail Plan projects

dependencies.text: Canonical rail-project IDs, delegation scope, sponsor acceptance and reimbursement, staffing, and candidate criteria

openQuestions: Which projects are eligible and who funds the review?
How are candidates prioritized against available review capacity?

directives.title: State and federal funding support for transit priority and complete trips
directives.excerpt: CalSTA, in coordination with Caltrans and the CTC, shall update state funding program guidelines to incentivize the delivery of transit priority infrastructure and first- and last-mile connectivity including, but not limited to, Solutions for Congested Corridor Program (SCCP), the Active Transportation Program (ATP), Local Partnership Program (LPP), and Transit and Intercity Rail Capital Program (TIRCP). Caltrans shall also identify federal funding programs that will support transit priority and first and last-mile connectivity efforts.

qualifiers.text: in coordination with Caltrans and the CTC
qualifiers.appliesTo: CalSTA update of state funding program guidelines
state funding programs whose guidelines are to be updated

analysis.summary: Update state program guidelines to incentivize transit-priority and first- and last-mile delivery, and identify federal programs that support those efforts.

expectedOutputs.text: Updated state program guidelines with defined incentives
A documented identification of applicable federal funding programs

dependencies.text: Program-cycle calendars, legal authority, shared definitions and measures, and federal eligibility rules

openQuestions: Which guideline cycles and owners apply?
How are transit priority, connectivity, and incentive magnitude defined?

directives.title: Transit-data guidelines and grant CRM
directives.excerpt: publish guidelines for consistent transit and passenger rail data availability

qualifiers.text: implementing standards that allow for efficient integration between systems
qualifiers.appliesTo: guidelines for consistent transit and passenger rail data availability

qualifiers.text: ensuring that consistent information is available to the public
qualifiers.appliesTo: transit and passenger rail data availability

qualifiers.text: so that grantees receive streamlined, coordinated communications from a single source
qualifiers.appliesTo: implementation of the grant Customer Relationship Management system

analysis.summary: Publish transit and passenger-rail data-availability guidelines through Cal-ITP and implement a coordinated grant CRM through CIMP in partnership with Caltrans IT.

expectedOutputs.text: Transit and passenger-rail data availability guidelines with standards and governance
A grant CRM covering availability, applications, management, and information campaigns

dependencies.text: Standards inventory, version governance, canonical grant schema, product ownership, integration, migration, privacy, accessibility, and language controls

openQuestions: Which data domains and standards are mandatory or advisory?
Is the CRM the system of record behind the funding dashboards, and what does “single source” mean operationally?

evidenceScope.coverageNote: This curated layer contains reviewed public artifacts selected for a documented relationship to a directive. It is selective, not comprehensive or live. Inclusion documents a source relationship; it does not establish implementation status, completion, compliance, or performance. Omission is not evidence that no implementation activity or public record exists.

evidence.title: Transit Executive Order N-7-26 Resource Material (7/15/2026)

accessibility.note: PDF metadata reports a tagged document; this record does not make an accessibility conformance claim.

evidence.editorialSummary: The Commission presents proposed changes to the draft 2026 Solutions for Congested Corridors Program and Local Partnership Competitive Program guidelines pursuant to Order 5 of Executive Order N-7-26.

locations: Page 1: Reference Instructions and SCCP Part IV, Section 17.2.2
Page 2: Reference Instructions; LPP-C Part II, Section 6B; and Part IV, Section 14.1

limitations: This record covers only proposed SCCP and LPP-C changes associated with Section 5's state-guideline clause. It does not cover ATP, TIRCP, other programs, or Caltrans's federal-funding-identification clause, and it does not establish adoption, completion, compliance, or agency performance.

This pre-workshop reference material retains a scheduled-event date because that is how the artifact framed July 15 when retrieved. A separately reviewed presentation and a recording were available on the Commission workshop page by July 22, 2026.
The artifact marks proposed additions with bold red styling, so a text-only extraction does not fully preserve that distinction.

evidence.title: 2026 SB1 Program Guidelines Development Workshop for Solutions for Congested Corridors Program (SCCP) and Local Partnership Competitive Program (LPP-C): Efficient Land Use and Housing Updates

accessibility.note: PDF metadata reports a tagged document; visual and text-extraction review found readable Order 5 slides, but this record does not make an accessibility conformance claim.

evidence.editorialSummary: The Commission's workshop presentation identifies Order 5 as the basis for proposed changes to the 2026 SCCP and LPP-C guidelines, reproduces the proposed transit-priority and first- and last-mile additions, and says guideline development is not currently open for the other Commission programs named by the order.

locations: Page 12: California Executive Order N-7-26 overview and current guideline-cycle note
Page 13: proposed SCCP Part IV, Section 17.2.2 accessibility addition
Page 14: proposed LPP-C Part II, Section 6B eligible-project addition
Page 15: proposed LPP-C Part IV, Section 14.1 accessibility addition

limitations: The presentation documents proposals discussed in one workshop; it is not a final guideline, adoption record, completion finding, compliance determination, or performance measure.

It addresses only the SCCP and LPP-C state-guideline clause. The statement that other Commission guideline cycles are not currently open does not establish when or how those programs will change, and the deck does not address Caltrans's federal-funding-identification clause.

The artifact header shows the July 15 workshop date, while PDF metadata shows the file was created on July 16 and the context page had added both the presentation and a recording by the July 22 Atlas review. The exact upload time is not stated.
Red styling identifies proposed additions on pages 13 through 15, so plain-text extraction does not fully preserve the change markup.
